phases.name: Parse weave-label and condition (front of pipeline)
phases.description: Thinnest end-to-end slice: teach the parser and AST to carry a choice (label) and a {condition} guard, so downstream emitters have the data to act on.

steps.name: Parse choice (label) and {condition} guard
criteria: A choice line with a leading (label) parses; the label text is captured, not left in choice content.
A choice line with a {condition} guard parses; the condition expression is captured separately from choice content.
A plain choice line with neither (label) nor {condition} parses unchanged (no regression).
splitBracketedLabel is reused with ( and ) delimiters for the choice label; no new parsing helper is added.
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM: example-based Swift Testing oracle + AST-assertion tests per CLAUDE.md (OOP paradigm; PBT N/A - DWD-9). Backtick function-name test style is MANDATORY; the @Test("string") label form is FORBIDDEN. EXTEND (WL-D2): InkParser.choiceKind parses a leading (label) (reuse splitBracketedLabel with (/) exactly as gathers do) and a trailing/leading {condition} guard on a choice line; CompilerAST choice case gains weaveLabel: String? + condition: InkExpression? (symmetric with gather's existing label). No AT re-enables here - this is the walking-skeleton front of the pipeline; assert via AST-shape tests that the choice node now carries label + condition. Boundary: Compiler/ touches no Engine/ (R1); no JSONSerialization (R5); no runtime/Decoder change (WL-D7).

phases.name: Label-keyed containers and name->path resolution
phases.description: Make labelled choice outcome containers addressable, register labelled-only label->absolute-path entries via a discovery pre-pass, then emit .readCount on a label-resolved dotted reference.

steps.name: Key labelled choice outcome containers by label
criteria: A choice carrying a (label) keys its outcome container by that label instead of the positional c-N.
A choice with no label keys its outcome container by c-N as before (no regression).
The label becomes the addressable segment in the container's absolute compiled path.
Keying reuses the existing gatherKey idiom (label ?? c-N); no separate keying path is introduced.
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM: example-based Swift Testing oracle + AST-assertion tests per CLAUDE.md (OOP paradigm; PBT N/A - DWD-9). Backtick function-name test style is MANDATORY; the @Test("string") label form is FORBIDDEN. EXTEND (WL-D3): WeaveEmitter keys a labelled choice's outcome container by label ?? c-N, mirroring the existing gather keying (gatherKey: label ?? g-N) at the same WeaveResolver.resolve keying site. No AT re-enables here; assert via AST-shape tests that a labelled choice container is reachable by its label segment in the absolute path. Boundary: Compiler/ touches no Engine/ (R1); no JSONSerialization (R5); no runtime/Decoder change (WL-D7).

steps.name: Discovery pre-pass and weaveLabelPaths table
criteria: A discovery pre-pass records labelled-only label->absolute-path entries into LoweringContext before expression lowering runs.
The pre-pass collects the SET of labels that are read-count-referenced for later flagging.
WeaveResolver.resolve writes label->path at its existing keying site, reusing the already-computed keyPrefix without re-deriving paths.
Forward references resolve: a label referenced before it is lowered still maps to its path.
Unlabelled containers (g-N/c-N) are NOT registered in the table (labelled-only addressability).
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM: example-based Swift Testing oracle + AST-assertion tests per CLAUDE.md (OOP paradigm; PBT N/A - DWD-9). Backtick function-name test style is MANDATORY; the @Test("string") label form is FORBIDDEN. EXTEND (WL-D5, = inklecate's ResolveWeavePointNaming): add weaveLabelPaths: [String:[String]] to LoweringContext (mirroring the CONST/function table-threading pattern); add a discovery pre-pass over the parsed weave structure (reuse WeaveParser level-partitioning) that records labelled-only label->absolute-path AND collects the read-count-referenced label SET, both BEFORE expression lowering for forward-reference tolerance. WeaveResolver.resolve writes label->path at its existing keying site, reusing keyPrefix (never re-derives - the one thing inklecate avoids). No AT re-enables here. Boundary: Compiler/ touches no Engine/ (R1); no JSONSerialization (R5); no runtime/Decoder change (WL-D7).

steps.name: Parse dotted refs; emit .readCount for weave-label and knot/stitch read-counts
criteria: The expression parser accepts a dotted identifier (a.b) as a variableReference; a plain identifier is unchanged (no regression).
lowerExpression emits .readCount(resolvedPath) for a dotted reference resolving to a weave label OR a knot/stitch absolute path.
A dotted name resolving to no known label/knot/stitch falls through to .variableReference (not an error).
No dotted .variableReference survives lowering for a name that is a known weave label, knot, or stitch.
The dotted read-count RED-pin acceptance test (waiting.guard_post, knot.stitch) passes and its .disabled trait is removed.
steps.scenario_name: a dotted read-count reference to a named stitch lowers to a read-count node
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM: example-based Swift Testing oracle + AST-assertion tests per CLAUDE.md (OOP paradigm; PBT N/A - DWD-9). Backtick function-name test style is MANDATORY; the @Test("string") label form is FORBIDDEN. SCOPE EXPANDED (DELIVER back-propagation 2026-06-15, user-approved): ADR-011 scoped only weave-label read-counts, but the TheIntercept e2e also requires (a) parsing dotted identifiers in conditions and (b) knot/stitch read-counts (back_of_hut_2, time_to_move_now are stitches). Both fixable inside Compiler/. (1) PARSER: InkParserExpressions.isIdentifier (line ~155-159) currently rejects '.', so {a.b: ...} throws unexpectedToken before lowering; allow a dotted identifier (the lexer already keeps '.' in the token, line ~284) so it parses to .variableReference("a.b"). (2) RESOLUTION (= inklecate's ResolveReferences): in RuntimeObjectEmitter.lowerExpression .variableReference(name), resolve a dotted name against weaveLabelPaths AND knot/stitch absolute paths (register knot.name and knot.stitch paths the same way - reuse existing namedContent path arithmetic, never re-derive), emitting .readCount(resolvedPath); a true miss falls through to .variableReference (VariableReference.cs:129-141 parity - not an error). RE-ENABLES the RED-pin AT 'a dotted read-count reference to a named stitch lowers to a read-count node' (line ~133) - remove its .disabled trait as part of GREEN. Do NOT set the 0x1 CountVisits flag here (that is 03-01). Boundary: Compiler/ touches no Engine/ (R1); no JSONSerialization (R5); no runtime/Decoder change (WL-D7).

phases.name: Count-visits flag, condition lowering, and e2e ceiling
phases.description: Set the 0x1 CountVisits flag on exactly the read-count-referenced labelled containers, lower a {condition}-guarded choice's guard before its choicePoint, and re-enable the TheIntercept e2e ceiling test.

steps.name: CountVisits flag, condition lowering, and parser fixes
criteria: ContainerNode.flags = 0x1 (CountVisits) is set on exactly the read-count-referenced targets (labelled weave containers AND referenced knots/stitches) from the discovery SET.
Targets that are never read-count-referenced keep flags = 0 (no over-flagging).
A {condition}-guarded choice lowers its guard onto the eval stack before its choicePoint, reusing the lowerExpression idiom.
Parser: a `- -> target` gather-then-divert parses as a single-level gather whose outcome is a divert (consumeMarkers stops before a `->`).
Parser: a `text[]suffix` empty-bracket choice splits into printed text + glued suffix body.
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM: example-based Swift Testing oracle + AST-assertion tests per CLAUDE.md (OOP paradigm; PBT N/A - DWD-9). Backtick function-name test style is MANDATORY; the @Test("string") label form is FORBIDDEN. SCOPE EXPANDED #2 (DELIVER back-propagation 2026-06-15, user-approved 'deliver full e2e'): the full TheIntercept fixture surfaced two surgical InkParser.swift bugs blocking compile (both parser-only, in-boundary): (1) consumeMarkers counts the '-' of a '->' divert as a 2nd gather level, so 'level-1 gather then -> divert' (e.g. '- -> target') mis-levels - fix: stop consuming a '-' marker when the next char is '>'; (2) a 'text[]suffix' empty-bracket choice-only split is not handled. InkParser.swift added to files_to_modify. SCOPE EXPANDED #1: flagging covers referenced knots/stitches as well as labelled weave containers (the e2e reads stitch counts back_of_hut_2/time_to_move_now). EXTEND (WL-D4, generalised): set ContainerNode.flags = 0x1 (CountVisits) on exactly the read-count-referenced targets from the discovery SET - labelled choice/gather containers AND referenced knot/stitch containers (knots/stitches currently emit flags:0 at RuntimeObjectEmitter ~lines 405/412) - matching inklecate VariableReference.cs:101 (flag only resolved targets; countAllVisits OFF; over-flagging diverges from the oracle). EXTEND (WL-D2/WL-D8): a {condition}-guarded choice lowers its guard before the choicePoint, reusing ConditionalEmitter's lowerExpression idiom (pattern reuse - no edit to ConditionalEmitter). E2E DESCOPED from this step (user decision 2026-06-15: 'cut our losses; find an alternative approach'). Closing the TheIntercept e2e was found to require a THIRD subsystem beyond ADR-011 (variable-text {|...|} at a gather-lead position must thread back into the gather's nested choices - VariableTextEmitter + gather-lead continuation threading), with further unknown blockers past line 86. The e2e AT STAYS .disabled with a precise, evidence-backed reason; full closure is a follow-up feature, not a per-blocker chase. This step delivers the read-count addressing subsystem + flagging + condition lowering + the two surgical parser fixes, all regression-free. Boundary: Compiler/ touches no Engine/ (R1); no JSONSerialization (R5); no runtime/Decoder change (WL-D7).

validation.notes: APPROVED 2026-06-15, 0 blockers (initial review). SCOPE EXPANDED 2026-06-15 (user-approved DELIVER back-propagation): steps 02-03/03-01 grew beyond ADR-011's weave-label-only scope to also cover (a) the expression-parser dotted-identifier gap and (b) knot/stitch read-count addressing + flagging - both required for the TheIntercept e2e and both fixable inside Compiler/. ADR-011 + feature-delta updated with the finding. 5 production files / 5 steps = ratio 1.0; zero CREATE NEW; boundary R1/R3/R5 + WL-D7 unchanged.
